name: qchat
readme:
# Quarterly Check-Ins (QChat)

Multi-team quarterly check-in scheduling app. Managers create timeslots, members book check-ins, and calendar invites are sent via Microsoft Graph.

## Roles

- **Admin** — Full manager experience + user/team/quarter management + view-as any user
- **Director / People Manager** — Manager experience; optionally included as attendee on check-ins
- **Manager** — Creates timeslots, views dashboard for their team
- **Member** — Books one check-in per quarter from their manager's slots

## Local Development

```bash
# Start Postgres
docker compose up -d

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Configure environment
cp .env.example server/.env
# Edit server/.env with your settings

# Start dev servers (client + API)
npm run dev
```

Client runs at http://localhost:5173, API at http://localhost:3001.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `DATABASE_HOST` | Yes | Postgres host |
| `DATABASE_PORT` | Yes | Postgres port (default: 5432) |
| `DATABASE_NAME` | Yes | Database name (default: qchat) |
| `DATABASE_USER` | Yes | Database user |
| `DATABASE_PASSWORD` | Yes | Database password |
| `SESSION_SECRET` | Yes | Express session secret |
| `ADMIN_EMAIL` | Yes | Email of the initial admin user |
| `GRAPH_CLIENT_ID` | No | Azure AD app client ID |
| `GRAPH_TENANT_ID` | No | Azure AD tenant ID |
| `GRAPH_CLIENT_SECRET` | No | Azure AD client secret |

Leave `GRAPH_*` vars blank to disable auth and calendar integration for local dev.

## Data Migration from PPT QChat

```bash
npx tsx server/src/scripts/migrate-from-ppt.ts \
  --source-db "postgresql://user:pass@old-host:5432/ppt_qchat" \
  --manager-email "admin@company.com"
```

## Deployment

Docker image with Caddy (static SPA) + Node (API), port 8080. Deployed to Launchpad via `.deploy/` manifests and `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`.
